reason: Current industrial production level captures persistence and business-cycle state.
Recent IP growth captures production momentum.
Manufacturing IP is a major component of aggregate industrial production.
Capacity utilization measures output relative to sustainable capacity and is directly tied to industrial activity.
Average weekly hours in manufacturing are used as an LEI component and production-worker hours are an input to some Fed IP estimates.
Manufacturers' new orders proxy forward production demand and are an LEI component.
Real manufacturing and trade sales are part of coincident business-cycle conditions and demand for production.
Building permits lead construction-related materials and durable-goods demand.
Stock prices summarize forward-looking financial expectations for activity and demand.
Term spread summarizes monetary/financial conditions relevant for investment, goods demand, and production cycles.
